old_claim.note: Granaries provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: The paper defines threshing sledges as animal-traction machinery and reviews Early Neolithic cattle evidence compatible with traction, while acknowledging that early use may have been sporadic.

source_shape.source_locator: Introduction and discussion define sledges as animal-traction machinery, review Early Neolithic cattle traction evidence, and call early use potentially sporadic.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: Managed cattle supplied the traction pathway used by the scoped threshing-sledge machinery.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Animal management enables traction directly, whereas a granary is downstream storage and does not cause cereal separation.

ontology_before: Downstream grain storage was represented as an enabler of the earlier processing step.
ontology_after: The sledge links to the managed-animal capability that supplies its documented traction pathway.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: animal_husbandry replaces granaries as an enabling prerequisite.
would_reject_if: The Early Neolithic sledge interpretation were shown to involve no animal traction and to depend instead on granary infrastructure.
short_reason: Animal management enables sledge traction; granaries are downstream storage.
